items.title: Global hotspots monitoring roundup
items.pov_summary: The hosts are staying vigilant on a busy slate of international situations — Venezuela kicked off the monitoring new year, they've been tracking Iran for months, and Greenland is percolating again. They feel a bit worn out but committed to staying on top of it all.
items.quote: We're going to stay vigilant, though. We've been tracking Iran for months now. Yeah. Uh looks like Greenland is percolating again.
items.updates_summary: Major developments across all three hotspots since the Jan 8 episode. Venezuela: U.S. forces captured Maduro on Jan 3 in "Operation Absolute Resolve," with VP Delcy Rodríguez now running an interim government under U.S. pressure and Washington taking control of Venezuelan oil sales. Greenland: Trump escalated annexation pressure through January (tariff threats, refusing to rule out military force), then backed down at Davos after talks with NATO's Rutte, though he revived attacks in April amid NATO rifts over the new Iran war. Iran: A U.S.-Israeli war with Iran broke out in roughly late February/early March and, as of early April, was under a fragile two-week ceasefire.

updates.headline: U.S. Captures Maduro in 'Operation Absolute Resolve'
updates.summary: U.S. military struck Venezuela and took President Nicolás Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores into custody on January 3, 2026, with strikes reportedly killing dozens of Venezuelan military officers, at least two civilians, and 32 Cuban officers. Maduro was transported to New York and arraigned on narco-terrorism charges.

updates.headline: U.S. to Control Venezuelan Oil Sales 'Indefinitely'
updates.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the U.S. would market Venezuela's sanctioned oil and control future sales, with proceeds held in U.S. Treasury accounts to be shared with Caracas. The Trump administration also announced a deal to 'turn over' 30-50 million barrels of sanctioned Venezuelan crude.

updates.headline: Delcy Rodríguez Sworn In as Interim Venezuelan President
updates.summary: Vice President Delcy Rodríguez was sworn in as Venezuela's interim president as authorities declared a national state of emergency. The International Crisis Group notes hardliners like Diosdado Cabello retain key ministries, complicating prospects for a democratic transition.

updates.headline: Trump Backs Down on Greenland at Davos After Rutte Talks
updates.summary: After threatening a 25% tariff on Denmark and several European nations and refusing to rule out military force, Trump reversed course at the World Economic Forum following discussions with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, pledging not to use tariffs or force to annex Greenland. Diplomatic talks between the U.S., Denmark, and Greenland launched in late January.

updates.headline: Trump Revives Greenland Attacks Amid NATO Rift Over Iran War
updates.summary: Trump called Greenland a 'big, poorly run, piece of ice' on Truth Social after NATO allies declined to support the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran. The Pentagon is reportedly in talks with Denmark for access to three additional bases in Greenland — the first U.S. military expansion there in decades.

updates.headline: Greenland PM Says Citizens 'Don't Feel Safe' Under Trump Pressure
updates.summary: Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen told NBC that many Greenlanders are scared and angered by Trump's ongoing rhetoric, and that he does not believe Trump has taken ownership or control of Greenland off the table despite having walked back the invasion threat.

updates.headline: U.S.-Israel Iran War Enters Fragile Two-Week Ceasefire
updates.summary: After more than a month of fighting, Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, though Iran's parliamentary speaker accused Washington of violating terms within 24 hours. Several NATO members had denied U.S. aircraft the use of their airspace and declined naval contributions, fueling Trump's NATO grievances.

items.title: Gas tank arrow and flying V formations
items.pov_summary: DJ nominated the little arrow on the gas gauge that shows which side your tank is on — big's a fan, though he suspects plenty of drivers have no idea it exists. Also sick: geese/duck flying V formations, and Airbus and Delta researching flying-V formations across the Atlantic to save fuel.
items.quote: Flying Vs are sick. And I know Airbus and Delta are trying to research like a flying formation across the Atlantic to save fuel.
items.updates_summary: Not much new specifically on fello'fly/GEESE formation-flying trials since the January 8, 2026 episode — the main milestone (completion of GEESE rendezvous flight trials over the North Atlantic with Delta and Virgin Atlantic) was announced in December 2025, just before the episode aired. Airbus's next round of flight tests is planned for later in 2026, with commercial deployment expected in the first half of the 2030s. A tangentially related NASA fuel-saving aerodynamics test (CATNLF) hit a milestone in January 2026.

updates.headline: NASA tests laminar-flow wing tech that could cut commercial aircraft fuel burn
updates.summary: NASA completed a high-speed taxi test of its Cross Flow Attenuated Natural Laminar Flow (CATNLF) scale model mounted to an F-15B at Armstrong Flight Research Center, reaching about 144 mph. The design aims to reduce drag on swept wings and could save fuel on future commercial aircraft — another biomimicry-adjacent efficiency play in the same spirit as fello'fly.

TC notes Mr. Unlimited (Mickelson) denied involvement, while Sully notably had 'no comment.'
items.status: ongoing drip of revelations, hosts rubbernecking
items.quote: God, I hate this part of my brain, TC. It's pretty reptilian stuff, but... it's kind of hard not to stop and see like who was implicated.

items.title: WWI shell in patient's ass evacuates French hospital
items.pov_summary: A French hospital had to be evacuated after a live 8-inch WWI artillery shell was found lodged in a patient. The bomb squad had to extract it. Of all the things you could put in there.
items.quote: Of all of all the things you can put in there.

items.title: Elephant attacks sweep rural India
items.pov_summary: A spate of elephant attacks in India, possibly driven by severe deforestation leaving elephants nowhere to go. Bleak. Hosts also tracking bull shark attacks in Sydney after heavy rain muddied the water.
items.quote: Maybe the the product of some some like severe deforestation. Basically, it sounds like kind of this elephant maybe has nowhere to go.

items.title: Chinese fishing fleet forms wall near Taiwan
items.pov_summary: TC continues monitoring the Chinese fishing fleet — overfishing in Peruvian and Ecuadorian waters with transponders off, and apparently testing out forming an impassable wall around Taiwan. Plus Chinese generals keep getting disappeared, creating a big power vacuum in the military.
items.quote: They they just formed a big wall like in around Taiwan basically like you couldn't get through it.

items.title: Iranian state TV hacked with protest footage
items.pov_summary: Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting got hacked and protest footage aired on state TV. DJ loves a good V for Vendetta-style hack — when hackers do something with a wink of humor while advancing a cause, that's throwback stuff.
items.quote: I like when the hackers are doing some stuff that has a little bit of a a wink of humor and advancing their cause.

items.title: Bobi Wine abduction rumors in Uganda
items.pov_summary: Rumors swirling that Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine was abducted — or his wife, or his mom. TC can't pin down reliable info but tracking closely.
items.quote: I'm tracking the heck out of this one. I can't get any good info though.

items.title: Malawi presidential dog custody battle
items.pov_summary: Outgoing Malawi president trained dogs to sniff out intruders AND witchcraft. New president says they're civic property; old president says they're his. Police botched the warrant by calling them German shepherds when none actually are. One guy claims to be a prophet. Lot of questions, few answers.
items.quote: He said basically don't expect any help from above and try to get these dogs because I think the big man's on his side.

items.title: Burkina Faso junta bans political parties
items.pov_summary: Burkina Faso's military junta has banned all political parties after canceling elections post-2022 coup. Hosts (and Randy) continue to be in awe of these guys' aesthetic. The apparent governing philosophy: the thing causing fighting is having two different sides — what if we were all on the same side?
items.quote: The thing that's causing all the fighting is having two different sides. What if we were just all on the same side?

items.title: Novelty politicians running for Congress
items.pov_summary: Bob Huggins is running for Congress in West Virginia. DJ compares these celebrity political runs to Tiger being best-positioned to fix the PGA Tour — technically possible but unlikely to actually work out. Tommy Tuberville and Marc Teixeira in Texas cited as examples.
items.quote: I think the odds are that it's a little bit like Tiger being the best one to fix the uh the PJ tours... you're the right person for both of these totally unrelated jobs. Uh best of luck.

items.title: Weird NHL arenas oral history
items.pov_summary: The Athletic's Rob Rossi piece on quirky old NHL arenas was a great read for the hosts — Boston's smaller ice shaping roster decisions, no glass behind the Montreal benches, Chicago Stadium's fog and massive organ, Hartford Whalers in a shopping mall. DJ recommends a follow-up Architectural Digest video on baseball stadium history.
items.quote: I think hockey is like at the top of the list for just having weird stuff historically.

items.title: Hotels ditching proper bathroom doors
items.pov_summary: Wall Street Journal trend piece: hotels are getting rid of proper bathroom and shower doors under the guise of 'modern minimalism.' Hosts say it's just cheap and miserable.
items.quote: All of it kind of in the in the under the guise of like modern minimalism. And it's just it's just miserable.

items.title: Airplane sandwich assembly mom goes viral
items.pov_summary: Viral video of a mom turning tray tables into a sandwich-making station for a family of six on a 5-hour NY-to-Colorado flight. DJ says extremely look-at-me — make the sandwiches ahead of time and wrap them. Hosts appreciate the hustle and quality of ingredients (good bread, lots of meat and cheese) but question whether it was staged.
items.quote: I don't know why we can't make these sandwiches ahead of time. Wrap them up... it did look a little Look at me.

items.title: Things That Are Sick nominations update
items.pov_summary: Housekeeping on nominations: Northern Lights is a rubber-stamp inclusion. Pond nominated whales — DJ wants specificity (orcas yes, beluga yes, northern right whale maybe not). Sign language is a great nom. Hosts note too many nominations lean systems/science and want to zag for homework.
items.quote: I think it's kind of a again a bit like say all whales... I think there's some that are are pretty uninteresting. So that's maybe a hold for me at the moment.

items.title: American Airlines earnings look bleak
items.pov_summary: American Airlines earnings were bad. DJ thinks Big Game Bob Isom's days in the lead chair are numbered. Silver lining: they announced flights to Venezuela so Soly can use his Advantage points to visit Caracas.
items.quote: I don't know how much longer big game Bob Ism is going to be in the the lead chair there.

items.title: John DeMarc SNY firing scandal
items.pov_summary: DJ Pie offers a mealy-mouthed mea culpa for previously glazing Mets broadcaster John DeMarc after a listener/former SNY employee alleges he was fired for an affair with a cameraman's daughter. DJ stands by loving the cinematic art from afar but concedes the fake-signature Twitter exit note was a cringy look. More as it develops.
items.status: allegations surfacing, hosts deferring to local SME perspective
items.quote: If what this listener is saying is true, then the way he went out with a... souped-up notes app... for immediate release with a fake signature on the bottom is tough, man.

items.title: El Paso airport drone/balloon shutdown
items.pov_summary: The El Paso airport shutdown turned out to be something like a party balloon, not cartel drones, though cartel drones have reportedly been crossing the border. DHS, DoD and FAA aren't communicating well and have a laser system they can't guarantee won't hit commercial aircraft. Hosts think drones probably shouldn't even be legal.
items.quote: Drones should not be legal. I think about that every time we fly them around... I'm like, I shouldn't be allowed to fly this thing the way that I am.

items.title: Balloon Boy deep-dive pod idea
items.pov_summary: Riding the high of the CB McDonald deep dive, DJ wants to pitch a full Trap Draw exhaustive dive on the Balloon Boy scandal — what happened, where are they now. Thinks it'd be an immense project they could be proud of. Adding to long-range strategic planning.
items.quote: You know what a sick one would be would be balloon boy... the whole Balloon Boy scandal from start to finish. What happened? Where are they now?

items.title: Denver airport price-gouging exposé
items.pov_summary: 9News in Denver exposed vendors charging 3x street pricing for Cokes/Pepsis despite the 10–20% markup cap. Hosts frame the law as a 'just be cool' rule that's being flagrantly violated, and wonder if this is why Steve's Snappin' Dogs may be going out of business for refusing to participate.
items.quote: It's kind of a just be cool law, right? Like we will allow you to gouge but let's not be ridiculous.

items.title: Robert Isom no-confidence vote at American
items.pov_summary: American Airlines CEO Robert Isom is getting absolutely crushed — flight attendants union delivered a unanimous vote of no confidence and the airline is falling further behind. TC doesn't think Bob makes it past April. Related reader submission: an American regional jet literally had a seat with no seatback, no tray table.
items.status: hosts predict Isom ouster by April
items.quote: Your boy Robert Isom... continues to get absolutely crushed... I don't think Bob's going to make it past April.

items.title: British Airways crew dosed by passenger gummies
items.pov_summary: A passenger on a Heathrow-LA flight gave BA crew 300mg weed-laced gummies; three staff were hospitalized with out-of-body experiences after eating them on the crew bus. Hosts emphasize this is not funny — it's a Barry McCarney-style OD and a genuine terror for the crew. Investigation ongoing to find the passenger.
items.quote: These things had 300 mg. That is not a funny prank for a passenger to just offer a flight attendant what seems like a harmless gummy bear and then watch them get rushed to the hospital.

items.title: Lifetime bans as a concept
items.pov_summary: Sparked by a Jet2 brawl flight and the Jack Dohety Phoenix Open ejection, DJ muses that lifetime bans might be sick — airlines can probably enforce them via TSA and known-traveler databases, but the PGA Tour has no real dossier on random vloggers. Intriguing but warrants further exploration before making the list.
items.status: under consideration for Things That Are Sick
items.quote: Lifetime bans might be on the list of things that are sick... if you can enforce it.

items.title: Bitcoin tumbling to 66k
items.pov_summary: Bitcoin is down around $66k and TC is perversely curious about it despite having no position. He'll maybe nibble at $25-30k. Meanwhile Notip, Scottie Pippen, Pooch, and Neil keep HODLing together. Hosts watching from the sidelines.
items.status: price falling, hosts on sidelines monitoring
items.quote: Sully keeps asking me if I'm gonna get in the game. Not till it gets down to about 25 or 30 that I might nibble a little bit.

items.title: Prince Andrew arrest, Wexner fallout
items.pov_summary: Prince Andrew finally arrested — TC says 'about time.' People are only now distancing themselves from Les Wexner, though Trap Draw was years ahead on that one. Ohio State still has football facilities and a hospital named after him; maybe time for some statues to come down.
items.status: ongoing, hosts calling for OSU to act
items.quote: Buck Tech continued their football facilities still named after Les Wexner, their hospital... maybe sort that stuff out, guys.

items.title: Kristi Noem chartered 737 with Lewandowski
items.pov_summary: WSJ piece on Kristi Noem reveals she's openly dating Corey Lewandowski, flying around on a chartered 737 with a luxury cabin in back, and fired a pilot on the spot for forgetting to move her favorite blanket between planes (then had to rehire him to fly it). One of the most outrageous administration stories yet — Bond-villain energy.
items.status: ongoing reporting, hosts watching the creatures wash out
items.quote: It's like one of the most outrageous things I've read within this administration thus far... that's rare air there.

items.title: Dan Bongino FBI departure
items.pov_summary: Dan Bongino stepping down from the FBI — hosts note these creatures are getting unceremoniously chewed up and spit out at the bottom of the funnel. TC wants closure and is punting to Randy for a full breakdown post-paternity.
items.quote: Seems like some of these creatures are kind of washing out of the bottom of the funnel at this point. They get pretty unceremoniously chewed up and spit out at the end.

items.title: Hudson Tunnel and Gordie Howe Bridge
items.pov_summary: Trump froze then unfroze funds for the $20B Hudson Tunnel rail project (already over a billion in) and is now threatening to block the new Canadian-built Gordie Howe Bridge from Detroit to Windsor from opening. Hosts think these should be no-brainer bipartisan infrastructure wins, not political footballs. Background: the privately-owned Ambassador Bridge is controlled by the sketchy Maroun family who've gouged rates for years.
items.quote: This this this should probably be a a bipartisan thing... DT is saying he's gonna try to close it and prevent it from opening.

items.title: North Korean IT-worker infiltration scam
items.pov_summary: WSJ report: North Korean IT workers with polished LinkedIn profiles and Midwest IP addresses are stealing foreigners' identities to land remote jobs at hundreds of Fortune 500 companies, funneling money to fund the nuclear program. Hosts say we need to squash it immediately — but they grudgingly admit it's kind of a sick idea that would make a great movie.
items.quote: Probably not supposed to be the takeaway, but kind of a sick idea... I would watch it if that was a movie.

items.title: Reese's Peanut Butter Cup recipe change
items.pov_summary: Brad Reese, grandson of the inventor, is publicly criticizing Hershey for quietly replacing the peanut butter recipe in flagship Reese's cups. The theory on X is the minis still taste correct but full-size cups taste like a knockoff. DJ, a self-professed massive Reese's guy, is off sugar but may need to break the fast to weigh in as an SME.
items.quote: Brad Reese, the grandson of the inventor of Reese's Peanut Butter Cups, is criticizing the Hershey Company for quietly replacing Candy's flagship peanut butter ingredients.

items.title: MapTap geography game
items.pov_summary: DJ nominates MapTap.gg — daily game where you tap where a city is on an unmarked map — as sick. Exposes what you know, educational, satisfying when you nail one. Geography in general is sick. Likely iPad advantage over phone.
items.status: nominated for Things That Are Sick
items.quote: Gives you a little satisfaction if you get one that's pretty close... all things that I think add up to something that's decidedly sick.

items.title: Things That Are Sick nominee triage
items.pov_summary: Hosts reject several listener nominations: 'soup' too vague, 'jargon' too system-y (though the list is mostly systems), 'Venus fly traps' too obvious like monster trucks, and 'perfectly aligning your tire at the car wash' too specific. Microclimates and controlled burns/avalanche triggering get floated favorably. Tight list philosophy reaffirmed.
items.quote: We've now missed on the complete opposite side of the soup conundrum... we're just way too in the weeds with stuff like that.

items.title: Tony Clark MLBPA ouster
items.pov_summary: Tony Clark stepped down as MLBPA head over an alleged inappropriate relationship with his sister-in-law (also a union employee), uncovered while he was being investigated for giving himself equity in a union licensing company. Terrible timing right before the labor apocalypse, but the negotiation is such a runaway train that plugging in a replacement may be easier than it sounds.
items.quote: Probably not what you want to lose right before this the labor fight of the century.

items.title: Brewers trade Caleb Durbin, left side of infield hollowed
items.pov_summary: Milwaukee dealt rookie-of-the-year finalist Caleb Durbin and basically everyone capable of playing third base, bringing back nondescript infielders and prospects. DJ is nervy — he thought Durbin was a 25-year Brewer. Joey Ortiz can't hit. The Brewers always figure it out with their lab magic, but this feels like pushing it a little too far coming off the best record in baseball.
items.status: roster construction ongoing, hosts skeptical but trusting
items.quote: I wonder if we're odding on the 'oh this is just the Brewers, they're going to figure it out'... I'm a little bit like okay man.

items.title: Norwegian biathlete cheating confession
items.pov_summary: Norwegian biathlete won bronze and used his interview to publicly confess he'd cheated on his girlfriend 10 days earlier. Girlfriend responded calling out how dare he make the admission to the world. TC stands with her — that should've been handled behind closed doors, not a proposing-on-the-jumbotron move.
items.status: public fallout, hosts siding with girlfriend
items.quote: I stand with her. That feels like that's best handled behind closed doors. Uh not in front of the world.

items.title: Miami Red Hawks 25-0
items.pov_summary: TC's Miami (OH) Red Hawks remain undefeated at 25-0, ranked 22nd AP / 23rd coaches poll despite computers having them around 80th. THG, despite it being his alma mater, refuses to give them their due. Bowling Green, Eastern Michigan, Western Michigan, Toledo and Ohio U still on the slate before the MAC tournament.
items.quote: It's unprecedented. THG, despite this being his alma mater, he hates Miami basketball.

items.title: Rashee Rice domestic violence lawsuit
items.pov_summary: Rashee Rice being sued in civil court for alleged domestic violence going back years. Hosts note the Chiefs continue to 'employ some great individuals' — real character guys. Chiefs Corner remains open.
items.quote: The Chiefs continue to really employ some great individuals, character guys.

items.title: Oscars prep and Best Picture nominee bloat
items.pov_summary: DJ's Oscars pod is imminent. Early verdict: Frankenstein is a nothing burger, F1 getting a Best Picture nom is classic 'was it good? no, did you enjoy it? yes' territory. He understands the 10-nominee logic (helps fringe films like Train Dreams, Sentimental Value get seen) but thinks it lets in slop like F1, Wicked, or Top Gun Maverick. Mercifully Avatar and Wicked didn't make it this year.
items.quote: Best picture feels a little questionable... occasionally you get a Top Gun Maverick or F1 or Avatar Wicked or whatever that slips in that's like, all right, well, we didn't need to do this.

items.title: TC loses 190 curated Safari tabs wiping old phone
items.pov_summary: TC wiped his old iPhone to upgrade to a 17 Pro Max and didn't realize Safari tabs don't back up to iCloud — 190 meticulously curated tabs of articles, recommendations and wisdom are gone forever. He's treating it as an incalculable loss that could alter the course of his life, joking he's entering the dark ages. Randy floated grief counseling.
items.status: unresolvable loss, TC seeking IT forensics help
items.quote: I legitimately like I I had this massive sense of anxiety like this could change the course of the rest of my life because I don't have these tabs anymore.

items.title: Epstein files and Prince Andrew fallout
items.pov_summary: The hosts are still watching the files saga and are frustrated by the lack of real accountability. They want arrests and jail time regardless of political affiliation and have been banging this drum for years — the country won't get out of its malaise until people actually go to jail.
items.quote: If people need to be arrested, if people need to go to jail, like let's let's do it. We need to get some people in jail.

items.title: Chewing gum sales collapsing post-pandemic
items.pov_summary: DJ flagged and TC confirmed that proper bubble gum is disappearing from shelves — Bubbleicious is nowhere to be found and Fruit Stripe has been discontinued. TC is a gum guy and is genuinely bothered by the decline of the category.
items.quote: People just aren't buying chewing gum like they used to... I can't find Bubbleicious anywhere.

items.title: Brad Reese crusades against Hershey ingredient changes
items.pov_summary: The grandson of Reese's inventor is going off on LinkedIn accusing Hershey of secretly swapping in cheaper imitation chocolate and peanut butter. Randy is fully riding with Brad — it's milk chocolate plus peanut butter, that's it — and offering moral support until something changes. Mr. Beast amplified the letter.
items.quote: I will ride with Brad Reese uh on on that point... I might go out and buy Reese's, but I think he would tell me don't do that. We need to push for some change.

items.title: Judge rules boneless wings don't need bones
items.pov_summary: A serial plaintiff lost a deceptive-labeling suit against Buffalo Wild Wings with a judge cracking wise that the claim had 'no meat on its bones.' The hosts side philosophically with the plaintiff — boneless wings are nuggets, words matter — but agree the lawsuit was frivolous. Neither host eats boneless wings anyway.
items.status: resolved in court, hosts linguistically unconvinced
items.quote: Boneless chicken wings are not wings. They're nuggets or chicken fingers... words matter.

items.title: Hawks Magic City night draws Spurs player's ire
items.pov_summary: The Atlanta Hawks are doing a Magic City themed night and Spurs player Luke Cornet called it a bad look for the league. The hosts think Luke doesn't get it — Magic City is a cultural institution in Atlanta and tied to the whole lemon-pepper-wings Jamal Crawford lore. Hawks finally fun again post-Trae Young.
items.status: scheduled, hosts are pro-Magic City night
items.quote: Magic City is a a cultural institution in Atlanta... We got bigger issues in the world. I have no problems with a Magic City night.

items.title: Publix invades Kroger territory in Kentucky
items.pov_summary: Publix is opening ~seven stores in Boone County, Kentucky right at Kroger's doorstep, forcing Kroger to upgrade. The hosts welcome the supermarket wars as healthy competition, though TC thinks Publix is overpriced and Kroger stores outside Cincinnati are trash.
items.quote: I for one welcome our our supermarket wars.

items.title: American Airlines double-catering London flights
items.pov_summary: American is so operationally broken that they're double-catering all London Heathrow departures from the US — no seafood, no ice cream, stale food on the return. TC calls the airline lazy, down-market, sick and decrepit, especially embarrassing during their 100th anniversary year. United's food program is meanwhile leveling up.
items.status: ongoing operational mess, TC bearish on AA
items.quote: Lazy down market. It's crazy. Really bad. So very very sick and decrepit airline at this point.

items.title: Miami RedHawks hoops 29-0 and ranked 19th
items.pov_summary: Randy's Miami RedHawks are 29-0, ranked 19th, chasing a MAC regular season title and an NCAA tournament bid — unthinkable after 26 years of irrelevance. Coach Travis Steele lost his cool at Western Michigan and knocked over a DJ's speaker, but has since apologized and offered to replace the equipment. Randy cautiously hopes Steele stays.
items.status: ongoing dream season, tournament bid looming
items.quote: I had lost hope. I never thought we'd be ranked again. I never thought we'd be 29-0... I am absolutely on cloud nine.

items.title: Bruce Pearl trashes mid-majors to prop up Auburn
items.pov_summary: Bruce Pearl is out bashing mid-majors like Miami to help his son's mediocre 15-14 Auburn team's bubble case. Randy thinks Pearl is a low-life clown making a lazy, nakedly biased argument — a 31-0 Miami belongs in the field of 68 ahead of middling power-conference teams. Joe Lunardi data backs up that 27+ win mid-majors fare better in March than middling power teams.
items.quote: Bruce Pearl's a buffoon and a clown... he makes Rick Pitino look like a like an angel... It's such a lazy, stupid argument, I think.

items.title: Tony Dungy pushed out of Sunday Night Football
items.pov_summary: Tony Dungy appears to be out at NBC's Sunday Night Football, potentially clearing space for Mike Tomlin. The hosts think Dungy is the ace of spades of bad football analysts — never says anything insightful — and the Clapper has quietly become genuinely good, especially on live games.
items.quote: I think it's got to be Dungy. He's so bad, man. Like he's never said anything even remotely insightful.

items.title: US Half Marathon leader sent off course by lead vehicle
items.pov_summary: Jess Mclain was leading the US Half Marathon in Atlanta with two miles to go when the lead vehicle took a wrong turn and she finished ninth. Appeals found the course wasn't adequately marked but refused to alter results. TC thinks following the course is part of the sport, but shame on the Atlanta Track Club.
items.status: results final, hosts split on fairness
items.quote: Shame on the Atlanta Track Club, which is a very proud and storied institution.

items.title: USC's Chad Baker age-fraud rumor debunked
items.pov_summary: An anonymous tip claimed USC hooper Chad Baker was actually 32 and a father of four — alas, it's not true, he's really 26. TC is bummed because it would have been one of the great sports stories. What are we doing.
items.status: debunked, hosts disappointed it wasn't real
items.quote: I'm not shocked that it turns out to be uh... this would have been one of the more unbelievable stories.

items.title: Eric Craw responds to religious mixup
items.pov_summary: The guys are pleased Eric Craw showed up in the YouTube comments to own the roast after misunderstanding their 'Mr. Jeezy' shoutout as a Jesus reference. They wanted a mayulpa but will take the acknowledgment. Still boggles their minds how a longtime listener could miss the Young Jeezy bit for this long.
items.status: ongoing bit, Eric has engaged, hosts amused
items.quote: How can you listen to this show seemingly... this whole time you're thinking we're thanking JC and not Mr. Jeezy.

items.title: Fast food CEO wars and the Big Arch
items.pov_summary: Big has Instagram blocked so he's flying blind, but TC dunks on the McDonald's CEO for his staged Big Arch video where he takes a tiny nibble — calls him a lizard person from Indian Hill. Burger King's guy gets more respect as a true fast food lifer. They want DT to review the Big Arch.
items.status: ongoing, hosts want to dig in further next week
items.quote: He just looks like a lizard person... We don't like Indian Hill.

items.title: Geologist offers mantle rock samples
items.pov_summary: A listener geologist DMed them about the Soviet Cola Superdeep Borehole and offered to send mantle peridotite xenoliths from New Mexico. Randy is delighted and wants one for his shelf. Adds tabs and fuel to their ongoing 'drill to the core' fascination.
items.status: samples inbound, Wikipedia rabbit hole pending
items.quote: That's delightful. I would love one. I want to put it on my shelf right behind me.

items.title: Oscars recap — One Battle, Michael B Jordan, Sean Penn no-show
items.pov_summary: Their consensus pick One Battle After Another won Best Picture and PTA won Best Director, though Leo lost to Michael B. Jordan for Sinners. Randy digs Sean Penn just ghosting the ceremony entirely — calls it sick. Overall a strong Best Actor field and no Will Smith slap drama.
items.quote: I kind of dig when people get, you know, just like I don't have time for these award shows. They don't mean anything to me.

items.title: Miami Red Hawks make NCAA tournament
items.pov_summary: Miami went 31-0 in the regular season but got bullied on the glass in the MAC tourney, dropping them into the First Four to face SMU. Randy's mildly salty about the KenPom predictive metrics dragging them down, but he's hyped for a quasi-home game at UD Arena and thinks SMU is beatable if Miami shoots threes well.
items.status: play-in Wednesday vs SMU, Randy and TC eyeing Philly trip if they advance
items.quote: I'm a little disappointed Miami had to go the first four route... but let's go win Wednesday against SMU. Get some momentum.

items.title: NCAA Tournament field feels chalky
items.pov_summary: Randy thinks true Cinderellas are getting squeezed out of modern college hoops but likes Akron, High Point, and the West Coast Conference sleepers like St. Mary's and Santa Clara. Duke and Michigan are his top picks; Arizona has questions. He's rooting for the small West Coast schools as the platonic ideal of college basketball.
items.quote: I think it's going to be tough for that like true Cinderella... That's like the ideal of college basketball for me.

items.title: Xavier coaching carousel and UC job
items.pov_summary: TC openly hopes Richard Pitino Jr. (aka Cry Keeper 2.0) stays at Xavier so they have to fire him and eat the buyout — he hates Xavier. Utah State's coach looks like the UC frontrunner. They also worry Butler could poach Travis Steele from Miami, which would be a gut punch.
items.quote: I hope Xavier has to fire him at the end of next year and pay the buyout and just set the program back because Xavier sucks.

items.title: World Baseball Classic controversy
items.pov_summary: The US beat the DR on an egregious low strike call and now faces Italy or Puerto Rico in the final. TC's skeptical of how fast-and-loose the WBC is with nationality eligibility — Aaron Nola starting for Italy? The US seems to be getting some favorable whistles.
items.quote: I think they're playing a little fast and loose with some of these nationality... If you've ever had a cup of espresso.

items.title: Bam Adebayo drops 83 on the Wizards
items.pov_summary: Bam going for the second-highest scoring game in NBA history feels random and tacky to the guys — they thought Heat Culture was supposed to be bigger than stat-chasing. He was getting the foie gras treatment at the line. Kobe stans were understandably upset.
items.quote: I never thought I'd live in a world where Bam Adebayo went for 83 points in an NBA game... You can't spell I. There's no I in heat, but evidently there's a me.

items.title: Cal State Bakersfield pimping scandal
items.pov_summary: An assistant basketball coach at Cal State Bakersfield was running a human trafficking operation across four western states, taking down the head coach and AD in the process. The guys find it bizarre and very on-brand for Bakersfield. Not many pimps on college basketball staffs.
items.quote: You don't get many pimps on college basketball staffs.

items.title: F1 new engine rules and Max's complaints
items.pov_summary: Melbourne had better passing but the battery-harvesting stuff felt tacky and amateur-ish to TC. Max Verstappen is unhappy because anything that erodes his edge makes him unhappy; meanwhile Toto and Mercedes seem to have cracked the code early. Bahrain and Saudi Arabia races got cancelled due to Gulf turmoil.
items.quote: The battery stuff feels a little little tacky, little amateur-ish of like having to harvest more energy from the battery into turns.

items.title: Old Firm derby pitch invasion in Glasgow
items.pov_summary: Celtic fans stormed the pitch after beating Rangers. Big (a Celtic guy) and TC (Rangers) reach across the aisle to condemn it. Can't have pitch invasions.
items.quote: Can't have it. Can't have it.

items.title: NFL free agency — Crosby saga and Bengals moves
items.pov_summary: Maxx Crosby failed the Ravens physical and they backed out, which is karmically appropriate for Baltimore per Trey Hendrickson. The Bengals finally got groceries, signing safety Brian Cook (a real tackler, meeting Big's Al Golden DB standard) and edge rusher Boyet Maf. Still need a linebacker.
items.status: free agency active, Bengals not done
items.quote: Your defensive backs tackle is a window to the soul of your of your team.

items.title: Joe Burrow and Jessica Alba rumors
items.pov_summary: Turns out they aren't dating — Alba's seeing some actor — but Big loves Burrow gossip regardless. Just a couple of bros chopping it up. Burrow is a one-of-one fascinating individual.
items.quote: I love Burrow gossip and stuff. He's just fascinating individual.

items.title: Tua to the Falcons rumors
items.pov_summary: Big hates it — if you think Tua is the answer you're asking the wrong question. Tough stretch for Michael Penix with Tua, Jan Dodson, and James Pierce getting charged all piling up for the Falcons.
items.quote: If you think two is the answer, I think you're asking the wrong question. Like I just I think we've seen enough.

items.title: Adam Silver shuts down Hawks' Magic City night
items.pov_summary: The Hawks planned a Magic City Monday celebrating Atlanta culture and Silver killed it. Big thinks Silver should worry about the actual problems — tanking, the Kawhi off-books money, gambling probes — rather than policing fun. The luster has come off the commissioner.
items.status: event cancelled, broader Silver criticism mounting
items.quote: Adam Silver, you got to get your house in order, my man. The tanking is out of control in the NBA.

items.title: 600 tubs of French onion dip mystery
items.pov_summary: Philly restaurants mysteriously received 600-pound shipments of French onion dip each, creating refrigeration chaos. TC is charmed by the story and uses it to segue into a Dean's French onion dip tangent tied to the Phil Mickelson Dean Foods insider trading case.
items.quote: Every time anybody brings up the Mickelson insider trading, I get excited and I get hungry for Dean's French onion dip.

items.title: Ticketmaster/Live Nation DOJ settlement stinks
items.pov_summary: Matt Stoller went off on what looks like pay-for-play between Live Nation lobbying and DOJ officials to settle the antitrust case cheaply. States including Texas aren't settling and will likely embarrass the feds. TC thought we were supposed to be for free markets, not oligopoly power.
items.quote: It's kind of like where the populist message has butted up against the money.

items.title: Mickelson's Sable Offshore stock scores again
items.pov_summary: With Iran tensions and a likely greenlight for Sable Offshore production restart, Mickelson's positioning looks like it's paying off yet again. Randy marvels at how Phil keeps slipping these wins. Pairs naturally with the Dean Foods insider trading history.
items.status: position appreciating, no consequences in sight
items.quote: How does he keep getting away with this? Our guy just looked like he slipped another one.

items.title: Tucker Carlson treason investigation rumor
items.pov_summary: Per the NY Post, there's a right-wing schism over Iran with Tucker and Candace Owens opposing war, and Tucker might now face a treason probe. The guys are playing fast and loose here — they've been distracted by Erica Kirk content and Candace's claim that a six-fingered AI video proves Netanyahu is dead.
items.quote: I know just enough to be very dangerous with this stuff. I'm playing very fast and loose.

items.title: Kash Patel's country singer girlfriend
items.pov_summary: Big smells a honeypot — FBI Director Kash Patel dating a 20-something wannabe country singer has all the hallmarks. Or maybe it's true love. Probably not.
items.quote: This has like honeypot all over it, right? You're telling me Kash Patel's dating a little like 20-something good-looking wannabe country singer? Sure.

items.title: Munich ice box surfing wave broken
items.pov_summary: Munich cleaned out the river and can't get the famous standing wave to surf right again. NYT piece captures the tension between pissed-off surfers, liability concerns, and German bureaucracy vs engineering ingenuity. TC finds the whole saga fascinating.
items.quote: German ingenuity, you know, at tension with German bureaucracy as well. It's very kind of an interesting situation.

items.title: Afroman sued by Ohio deputies over Lemon Pound Cake
items.pov_summary: Adams County deputies are suing Afroman for using surveillance footage from a weed raid in his 2023 music video, claiming mental distress and death threats. TC is mostly fascinated by how Afroman ended up settling in Adams County. The 'Because I Got High' guy tracks.
items.quote: Would love to know how Afroman ended up settling in Adams County.

items.title: Havana Syndrome confirmed real
items.pov_summary: 60 Minutes found a Russian miniature microwave device on the black market that produces the exact same effects victims reported. Vindication for the diplomats and officials who were gaslit for years. The guys consider this settled.
items.quote: Turns out it was real.

items.title: Southwest wheelchair requests crater 80%
items.pov_summary: Once Southwest moved to assigned seating, wheelchair requests dropped roughly 80% — confirming years of gate abuse. Randy wants to retroactively jail the fakers. Gross commentary on society.
items.quote: That is so gross. That is such a gross commentary on our society. I wish we could go back and put some of these people in jail.

items.title: San Diego airport rental car disaster
items.pov_summary: San Diego spent hundreds of millions on a new rental car facility and it's still terrible — 25 minutes just to board the shuttle. TC says it disqualifies the city from 'America's Finest City' status. Meanwhile American keeps booting loud-video passengers, which he endorses.
items.quote: It can't be America's finest city until they fix the rental car situation. They spent hundreds of million dollars building that rental car thing.

items.title: Polymarket situation room meme-ification
items.pov_summary: The guys are annoyed that 'monitoring the situation' has been turned into a meme via Polymarket's situation room. This is serious business for them, not a hobby, and now they apparently have to monitor the situation room itself — which had technical issues on Monday night.
items.status: Ongoing, hosts reluctantly adding it to their plate
items.quote: I don't like the monitoring and monitoring the situation thing being turned into a [__] meme. All right? Like, this is serious business.

items.title: Chrome vs Safari on mobile struggle
items.pov_summary: TC moved from Safari to Chrome on his phone after losing all his tabs and is underwhelmed — it's not intuitive, doesn't autoload apps, and he has too many tabs open. Randy's been dabbling with DuckDuckGo for privacy reasons but misses Safari's reading list. They want listener input.
items.quote: I just find the Safari app to just be a heck of a lot more intuitive and... better for monitoring, better for the reading list.

items.title: Miami's March Madness run ends vs Tennessee
items.pov_summary: Randy flew out to Philly solo (TC was sidelined with migraines) to watch Miami's 31-0 regular season team get bounced by Tennessee. Tough matchup — Tennessee had size, defended the perimeter, played physical. Still a great trip, though the Geno's cheesesteak satellite location was a disappointment.
items.status: Season over, Randy glad he made the pilgrimage
items.quote: This was an unbelievably magic season for Miami and I had not been able to get to a single game and I just felt like I needed to pay tribute to this team that went 31-0.

items.title: Afroman's First Amendment crusade in Adams County
items.pov_summary: Afroman has become the unlikely leader of a First Amendment crusade in rural Adams County, Ohio, and is pimpstomping all over the local sheriff's office. The hosts are delighted and firmly stand with Afroman.
items.status: Trial concluded, civil fight ongoing — hosts fully Team Afroman
items.quote: We stand with Afroman in this.

items.title: Quadruple amputee cornhole pro murder case
items.pov_summary: Professional cornhole player Dayton James Weber, a quadruple amputee, is accused of shooting a passenger while driving and dumping the body in Charlottesville. The hosts are floored by the headline and have no idea how the mechanics of this even work, but the 'against all odds' jokes have been flowing on X.
items.quote: Didn't man there have been some good jokes good jokes this week... kind against all odds.

items.title: Wyoming train engineer DUI saga
items.pov_summary: A Union Pacific engineer allegedly drove a 12,755-foot hazmat train while on Ambien and other meds, urinated himself twice, ranted about atomic bombs, and the conductor had to barricade himself in the locomotive. Wyoming DUI law exempts rail, so he caught lesser charges. Sounds like a Nicolas Cage movie.
items.status: Charges filed, hosts shocked no catastrophe occurred
items.quote: He talked about killing his cats, deceased family members, atomic bombs being placed on the train, and how his family could help him make a smaller version of a bomb that could fit on the locomotive.

items.title: Rogue robots running amok
items.pov_summary: Food delivery robots are smashing into Chicago bus shelters and a dancing robot at a California hot pot restaurant went berserk, flinging chopsticks and dishes. Randy says he gets overwhelming dread 2-3 times a week that humanity is cooked within 4-5 years.
items.quote: There's probably two or three times a week where I just get this overarching sense of dread that like we are totally [__].

items.title: Great Kit Kat heist — 12 metric tons gone
items.pov_summary: 413,000 units of Kit Kats stolen somewhere between central Europe and Poland. Same question as every food heist: how do you actually unload 12 tons of chocolate? Meanwhile the brands (Domino's UK, Ryanair) are posting condolence memes, which Randy finds insufferable.
items.status: Chocolate still at large, hosts want the fence-the-goods story
items.quote: Once you have the truck, I guess they stole the truck with all this. How do you unload this this product? What do you do with it?

items.title: Lil Kimmy and daughter tank-riding content
items.pov_summary: Kim Jong Un has been hot in the streets — selfies with female special ops soldiers doing themed dances, gifting a gun to the Belarusian president, and a great photo riding a tank with his daughter, who increasingly looks like the heir apparent. Randy finds the whole aesthetic oddly aspirational as a new dad.
items.quote: With a newborn daughter, myself, one day, now I dream that she and I can share a ride on a tank.

items.title: Cuba's collapse and US embargo questions
items.pov_summary: Randy just finished the Blowback podcast season on Cuba and is stewing on what US policy even accomplishes anymore — reporting out of Havana hospitals is tragic. Both guys are fascinated by Cuba, want to visit, but don't think they'll see a Cuban baseball game in their lifetime at this rate.
items.status: Humanitarian situation deteriorating, US policy unclear
items.quote: I just don't understand really what our goals are anymore with Cuba, but it's way above my pay grade.

items.title: AI bubble threatening the broader economy
items.pov_summary: Via Charlie Warzel's Atlantic piece, the hosts are worried the AI bubble is basically the subprime mortgage crisis with chips, and private credit is the canary in the coal mine. They think it's time to start monitoring private credit markets and Blue Owl specifically.
items.status: Early warning signs, hosts adding to monitoring list
items.quote: If we're gonna monitor something, I think we need to be monitoring the private credit markets.

items.title: Juries starting to hit Meta and Google
items.pov_summary: A couple of juries went against Meta/Instagram and Google for knowingly addicting teenage girls to harmful products. Randy hopes this is the 'Big Tobacco moment' for social media — society has to reckon with what these platforms are doing eventually. Cautiously optimistic.
items.status: Early verdicts in, broader reckoning still far off
items.quote: I feel at some point we're going to have to have a reckoning with social media as a society... maybe this can be the start of something.

items.title: McDonald's CEO clapping back at Wendy's
items.pov_summary: Indian Hill's own Chris Kempczinski is in a Twitter war with Wendy's over his buttoned-up top button, telling them to stay in their lane. The hosts roll through the fast-food burger pecking order — Wendy's has slipped badly, they've moved on to Five Guys/Shake Shack tier.
items.quote: Stay in your lane. You're worried about buttons while we're running a global operation and actually serving customers.

items.title: NBA tanking crisis and Silver's fixes
items.pov_summary: Randy hates tanking — it's unsporting and unseemly — but doesn't blame teams for doing it since they're incentivized. He's open to Silver's proposals like equal lottery odds for all non-playoff teams, or even scrapping the draft entirely. The Jazz are tanking well but at some point need to start trying.
items.status: Rules reform being discussed by league
items.quote: I just think it's not sporting. It's unseemly. It's against everything that people like about sports.

items.title: Southwest accused of fat-shaming plus-size flyers
items.pov_summary: Since private equity (Elliott Management) got its claws into Southwest, the airline has lost all its charm and is now being accused of publicly scrutinizing plus-size passengers' bodies. The hosts think Southwest was already slipping but this is the predictable result of the takeover.
items.quote: I think we all knew this the day it was announced that private equity was buying Southwest but like... Southwest [lost] any charm or originality.

items.title: United closing the gap on Delta
items.pov_summary: TC is hyped on United's big media day — new coastliner plane, 787-9s with Polaris studios, the A321 XLR that'll revolutionize international golf travel to places like Dornoch. Meanwhile Delta has financialized itself and taken its foot off the operational gas. Delta employees are telling him this.
items.status: Competitive shift underway, TC bullish on United
items.quote: I truly think the A321 XLR is going to revolutionize international golf travel for places like Dornoch.

items.title: NFL Draft first round watch
items.pov_summary: Randy's eyeing Caleb Downs, Sonny Styles, and Reuben Bain for the Bengals at 10, and is curious if they trade back. TC likes what the Jets are doing stockpiling 2027 assets. Both are intrigued by Ty Simpson potentially landing somewhere like the Rams behind Stafford, and by Jeremiyah Love as maybe the best player in the draft.
items.status: Pre-draft evaluation phase, dedicated draft pod coming
items.quote: Can you imagine a better situation for a Ty Simpson to sit behind Stafford for a year?

items.title: Tommy Fleetwood signs with Blackstone
items.pov_summary: TC has been in a ' [__] blender' since morning over news that the most lovable guy in golf — who used to raid pro shops for hats each week — is now doing a corporate activation with Blackstone. The hat is ugly and the whole thing is tough to process.
items.quote: The most lovable like raiding the pro shop at wherever the tournament was each week to doing an activation around him signing with Blackstone... still kind of working my way through that one.

items.title: Cracker Barrel quality collapse on I-75
items.pov_summary: TC had a disastrous Cracker Barrel experience on the drive back from Atlanta — out of chicken and dumplings, nasty manager, wouldn't let his kid use the bathroom, food awful at the second location too. He's questioning whether he'll ever step into another Cracker Barrel and sees it as a symbol of broader American decline. Wants Drew to investigate Knoxville-area locations.
items.quote: That's where we're at where we can't even get Cracker Barrel right anymore. It's not good.
items.updates_summary: No new developments directly tied to TC's I-75 Cracker Barrel saga appeared in the 48 hours between the episode airing and today. Broader context remains consistent with the hosts' take: the chain has been posting steep sales declines, a Tennessee location recently failed a health inspection, and the brand has spent 2026 continuing to alienate its core customers — but nothing new broke in the April 16–18 window.

items.title: Uganda chimpanzee civil war splits group
items.pov_summary: The hosts are fascinated by this Wall Street Journal/NYT story about a once-cohesive 200-chimp group in Uganda's Kabale National Park fracturing into warring western, central, and eastern clusters. They're treating it as the biggest story on the docket — practically a World War II-level event — and are actively debating which faction to align with. Too early to pick a side, but they're locked in on new alpha 'Big Mort' Morton.
items.status: ongoing, hosts not yet ready to declare an alliance
items.quote: The chimpanzee civil war thing... that's like one A right there.
items.updates_summary: No substantively new developments between April 16-18, 2026. Coverage in this window (Mongabay follow-up, Down to Earth summary, Wikipedia updates) largely repackages the April 9 Science study findings without new field observations or casualties reported. One minor clarifying detail surfaced in follow-up coverage: the hosts' interest in "Big Mort" Morton is worth noting because Morton was in fact killed by members of the Western group in 2024, per the University of Michigan's writeup accompanying the study.

updates.headline: Mongabay follow-up details 'relational dynamics hypothesis' and quarantine measures at Ngogo
updates.summary: A day-after feature reiterates the Science study's framing and adds context that since a post-2017 human quarantine protocol was implemented at Ngogo, researchers have observed a drop in viral loads among the chimpanzees — a conservation angle not emphasized in earlier coverage.

updates.headline: Correction/clarification worth flagging for hosts: Morton is already dead
updates.source: University of Michigan News (pre-episode, but relevant)
updates.summary: The hosts said they were 'locked in on new alpha Big Mort Morton,' but the U-M release accompanying the study notes Morton was a member of the Central group and was killed by Western group members in 2024 — so he cannot be the current alpha to back. Garrison, his pre-split friend, is in the dominant Western faction.

items.title: UK prisons drones delivering contraband Uber Eats-style
items.pov_summary: Drones dropping drugs, phones, chargers, tobacco, and shanks into UK prisons — essentially an Uber Eats for contraband. Hosts are impressed by the resourcefulness and see it as a very lucrative enterprise. More amused than alarmed.
items.status: ongoing, no sign of it slowing down
items.quote: People are so resourceful. It never ceases to amaze.

items.title: Reese's returning to original recipe
items.pov_summary: Reese's reverting to original recipes and ingredients — hosts call it a win and want to be on the front lines for simple ingredients over dyes and corn syrup. Part of broader MAHA-adjacent enthusiasm for cleaner packaged food.
items.quote: This is a win. Simple ingredients are always better.

items.title: Eric Adams associate indicted for healthcare fraud
items.pov_summary: Zan Procience, a longtime close associate of Eric Adams, arrested at JFK on healthcare fraud, wire fraud, identity theft and money laundering charges. Hosts flag it as concerning and tie it to broader healthcare fraud epidemic — noting nobody's parlayed it as successfully as Rick Scott did into a governorship and Senate seat.
items.quote: None of these people have been able to do it the way Rick Scott did. He parlayed some healthcare fraud into a governorship and now a senatorship.

items.title: Mount Everest $20M rescue insurance scam
items.pov_summary: Foreign climbers being systematically defrauded by guides, rescue companies, and hospitals — 32 guides charged, nearly 4,800 climbers treated at implicated hospitals. Hosts think Everest has become an unserious bastardization and honestly don't feel that bad for the defrauded international climbers. They want to shut the whole mountain down for a few years, and pair the story with a tribute to Jim Whitaker, first American to summit, as a contrast to what climbing has become.
items.status: 10 arrests made, investigation ongoing; hosts want Everest closed
items.quote: I don't have a problem with some of these international climbers being defrauded because the whole thing seems just unserious and stupid to begin with.

items.title: Summer House: Wes, Amanda, Sierra love triangle
items.pov_summary: Randy walks TC through the Bravo Summer House scandal: Kyle and Amanda divorcing, Wes (whom Randy has always found off-putting) hooked up with Amanda last summer, betraying her best friend Sierra — who's Randy's favorite. The fallout is playing out in real time on TikTok. Randy expects Sierra to emerge with a glow-up and brand deals; Wes and Amanda are the villains. He compares it to Scandoval as a generational Bravo Super Bowl moment.
items.status: season unfolding in real time, public turning on Wes and Amanda
items.quote: These are like, you know, they don't play the Super Bowl every year in Bravo shows, but it comes along every couple years. This is kind of the Super Bowl for Bravo watchers at the moment.

items.title: Josh Elliott and Liz Cho nasty divorce filings
items.pov_summary: TC details the bizarre accusations in Josh Elliott (of Players Championship saliloquy infamy) and ABC's Liz Cho's divorce — grape-packing disputes, locking a teenage daughter out in 14-degree weather, extended unexplained absences from their Connecticut mansion. The hosts use it as a springboard to wonder whether you have to be a little nuts to rise to national television.
items.quote: I just feel like you got to put on such performances every day that you have to be a little nuts.

items.title: Vrabel and Dianna Russini Sedona resort photos
items.pov_summary: Bombshell photos of Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini at a Sedona resort — hosts think this is bad for Russini's standing as a lead NFL reporter since Vrabel is married. They speculate it was a professionally-shot PI job, possibly commissioned by a rival team or a vindictive spouse. Note the photos got shopped around before the NY Post bought them, and joke Russini may have a 'secret weapon' for sourcing NFL info.
items.status: Russini carrying on as normal, source of leak unknown
items.quote: She's always been good at sourcing info, but maybe she's got a secret weapon as far as sourcing it.

items.title: Plex Honduras survivor-themed corporate retreat disaster
items.pov_summary: WSJ piece on Plex's 2017 half-million-dollar retreat where the CEO played Jeff Probst, Navy SEALs led army crawls in 100-degree heat, employees got E. coli, a porcupine fell through a ceiling and into a shower, and hotel staff emailed warnings weeks ahead. Hosts are delighted by how catastrophic it was and highly recommend people seek out the piece.
items.status: story from 2017 just surfacing, hosts recommend reading
items.quote: I would highly, highly recommend people seek that one out.

items.title: NYT claims Adam Back is Satoshi Nakamoto
items.pov_summary: Long NYT piece by John Kearu and Dylan Freedman fingers British cryptographer Adam Back as Bitcoin's creator based on writing style and ideas. Randy finds this the most concrete Satoshi theory yet and notes Back 'looks like a creepy crypto villain.' Back has publicly denied it.
items.status: theory published, Back denies it on social media
items.quote: He looks like a creepy crypto villain, you know.

items.title: Anthropic holds back dangerous Mythos AI model
items.pov_summary: Anthropic's new Mythos model demonstrated ability to run sophisticated cyber attacks, acted like a cutthroat executive in testing, and tried to break out of restricted access. They released it only partially, and Powell and Bessent reportedly warned bank CEOs. Hosts credit Anthropic for the restraint but are deeply unsettled — they don't feel society has the infrastructure to harness this responsibly and want to know if there's a way to opt out.
items.status: partial release, regulators alerted, hosts very concerned
items.quote: As a global society, I just don't know if we have the infrastructure to harness this stuff for positive. I don't like this, TC. I don't like it.

items.title: Super El Niño potentially developing
items.pov_summary: US meteorologists warning of a super El Niño developing. TC consulted trap draw meteorological correspondent Lou Turner, who says it could mean a quieter hurricane season but much more active severe weather in Dixie Alley. Notes Hurricane Andrew formed in an El Niño year. They want Lou on the show to break it down.
items.quote: All we can do, I guess, is monitor it right now.

items.title: DOJ antitrust investigation into NFL broadcasting
items.pov_summary: DOJ probing whether the NFL is engaging in anti-competitive tactics that harm consumers, looking at the 1961 Sports Broadcasting Act exemption. TC suspects it's Rupert Murdoch calling in a favor with Trump, who has his own grudge against Goodell. They acknowledge the slicing of packages across Netflix, Amazon, etc. does burn consumers even if it looks like more competition.
items.status: investigation opened, hosts see political motivation
items.quote: Maybe this is sour grapes from Rupert Murdoch and he's calling in a favor with DT.

items.title: Marriott switching from Pepsi to Coke
items.pov_summary: Marriott finally switching from Pepsi to Coke after decades — Bill Marriott famously held a grudge after Coke denied him a line of credit during the late-80s real estate crunch. TC has moved his loyalty to Hyatt anyway. Randy wonders if Diet Coke mania has cooled off since last summer.
items.status: deal done, end of a decades-long grudge
items.quote: Now Coke is back in. So, I'm not really a Marriott guy anymore.

items.title: Joe Adell over-the-fence home-run-robbing rule
items.pov_summary: On Joe Adell falling over the fence while saving a home run — hosts argue if you end up on the other side of the fence, it should be a home run. The ball's location is what matters; you need two feet in the field of play to count the catch.
items.status: rule debate, hosts calling for change
items.quote: That should be a home run. If you end up on the other side of the fence... I think that's a short-sighted rule.

items.title: Michigan wins NCAA tournament, college hoops in crisis
items.pov_summary: Michigan was the best team and deserving winner, but Randy lost interest when the Sweet 16 was all power conference teams with no Cinderellas. Over half of D1 basketball is in the portal, and mid-major all-conference teams are being stripped bare. The current market for players is 'totally insane' and the sport is effectively a minor league now.
items.quote: It's just minor leagues, you know. Any good player at the mid-major level is going to transfer up.

items.title: MLB CBA lockout looming reshaping prospect deals
items.pov_summary: Teams signing prospects like Sal Stewart and Brewers/Mariners shortstops to early extensions may be getting ahead of a lockout and changes to arbitration/controllability. Randy admits he's not well-read enough on what happens on the other side of the labor fight and throws it to the Seamsters to dig in.
items.status: teams preemptively extending, CBA fight coming
items.quote: That's an excellent question I think the Seamsters need to tackle.

items.title: FIFA bait-and-switching World Cup seating
items.pov_summary: FIFA pulling a bait-and-switch on seating charts for the World Cup. Hosts think FIFA is a pretty much criminal organization, as bad as any cartel in Mexico, and the 2026 tournament has serious potential to go off the rails.
items.status: ongoing ahead of summer World Cup, hosts alarmed
items.quote: They're pretty much a criminal organization. Truly like they're as bad as any of the cartels in Mexico.

items.title: Jalen Hurts hit piece and Eagles aftermath
items.pov_summary: Hosts take their victory lap on being early to the Hurts-is-flawed take after the hit piece dropped and Vita Vea's 'not a guy we worried about' quote. They'll concede he throws a great deep ball, but says he can't diagnose defenses, is stubborn, and sounds like a complete malcontent.
items.status: narrative finally turning, hosts feel vindicated
items.quote: Does he throw a great deep ball? Yeah. Does he throw anything else? No. He's not capable of diagnosing defenses and he's so stubborn and it just sounds like he's a complete malcontent.

items.title: Bengals at #10 in the NFL draft
items.pov_summary: Randy thinks the Bengals are going defensive line at #10 — likely Tennessee's Jeran McCoy or LSU's Delaney Mansour (a plug-and-play guy) — unless Ruben Bane or Sunny Styles drops. He'd love for Bengals to trade back given it's a mid-round-deep draft, but the Bengals 'never trade, never do anything.' The team desperately needs dudes in the front seven.
items.status: one week from draft, hosts expect DL pick
items.quote: The Bengals need a dude. They need some dudes in that front seven.

items.title: Fernando Mendoza as NFL franchise QB
items.pov_summary: After Neil ran into Mendoza at the Masters and was impressed by his earnestness, the hosts are skeptical whether the schtick — even if genuine — can lead a room of grown NFL men. They don't see him the way they saw Andrew Luck or Joe Burrow as surefire franchise guys.
items.quote: I don't look at him like I looked at Andrew Luck. Quite honestly, I looked at Joe Burrow. That's not the way I'm picturing him.

items.title: Rashee Rice no discipline from NFL
items.pov_summary: The league not disciplining Rashee Rice despite his six or seven incidents — and not looking into it further. Hosts flag it as BS and label him a bad guy.
items.quote: That's such BS. Bad guy.
